$comment: D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL-NONSYMBOL-PTR-MEMBER (c68, 2026-06-30): the post-c67 sqlite re-probe took the aggregate-rvalue guard (D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME) from 64->3 errors; the 3 survivors are sqlite's static FuncDef tables (aBuiltinFunc[]/aJsonFunc[]/aWindowFuncs[]) — aggregate globals with a member c67's tryClassifyAggregateConst could not fold. TWO fix-classes, both in src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp: (A) a NULL-BASE array-element address constant `(void*)&((char*)0)[X]` (sqlite's SQLITE_INT_TO_PTR — stash int X in a void*) = the integer X*sizeof(elem) as a pointer, NO relocation. New helper tryClassifyNullBaseIndexConst (sibling of c67's tryClassifyNullPointerConst): peel ptr cast(s) -> AddressOf -> Index(nullBase, X); base MUST fold to null (conservative — `&realArray[i]` returns nullopt and stays fail-loud, no over-fire); value = X * computeLayout(elemType).size. The array-element sibling of c43's offsetof MEMBER folding which the CST const-eval punted to the HIR/global-init engine. (B) a bare Ref to a file-scope `char[]` global decays to &arr[0] — a SYMBOL address (addend 0, abs64 reloc): tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr's bare-Ref arm extended from functionSymbols to ALSO accept a global whose Ref type is Array (scalar-global Ref stays an rvalue load, rejected). With both members classified, the whole aggregate folds to static .data + relocs (c67's path). Runtime verifies: pUserData round-trips to 7/11/0, zName points at 'alpha'/'beta'/'gamma' -> 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert either fix -> the member fails to classify -> the aggregate falls to runtime-init -> H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind (the aggregate-rvalue guard at hir_to_mir.cpp ConstructAggregate arm). gcc/clang exit 42.
